mechanism_narrative: A persistently high level of industrial production reflects sustained manufacturing strength, which supports corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 2% from its previous year level and SPX still has positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the 5% threshold, monetary policy is sufficiently restrictive to slow the economy and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If after a FEDFUNDS >5.0% trigger, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases over the walk-forward period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and falling housing starts indicate stagflationary conditions that weaken consumer purchasing power and construction activity, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If over the walk-forward period, the average SPX 20-day return after trigger is not negative with statistical significance (one-sided t-test p<0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 5%), an increase in the unemployment rate signals an imminent recession and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If within the regime (FEDFUNDS > 5%), the average SPX 20-day return after a rise in UNRATE is not negative, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A strong increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and rising income, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive short-term equity returns within a week.
failure_condition: If after the trigger (PAYEMS increase >150k), SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over the walk-forward period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales and industrial production increase concurrently, it signals synchronized demand and supply expansion, fueling corporate earnings and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If over the walk-forward period, the average SPX 20-day return after trigger is not positive with statistical significance (one-sided t-test p<0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6% (indicating recession), the federal funds rate becomes a powerful negative signal because in a weak economy, any rate increase further depresses activity and equity prices.
failure_condition: If within the regime (UNRATE > 6%), the rank IC of FEDFUNDS against SPX 20-day returns is not negative over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.5 million units, it signals a strong residential construction sector and housing market, which drives economic growth through multiplier effects, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after the trigger (HOUST >1500), SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over the walk-forward period, the hypothesis is rejected.
